title: Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of West Bengal

overview.short: Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary is in Bardhaman, West Bengal, India.
overview.description: Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary is in Bardhaman, West Bengal, India. The animals found include spotted deer and common langur. Ramnabagan Mini Zoo in the Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary spread over an area of 14.31 hectares is in Mouza Baburbag in Burdwan. This forest patch is under the control of Divisional Forest Officer, Burdwan Division who acts as Ex-Officio Director of the Zoo.

topPlaces.name: Bardhamanbhukti
topPlaces.description: Bardhamanbhukti was an ancient and medieval region/territory of Bengal spread across what are now Purba Bardhaman, Paschim Bardhaman and Bankura districts in the Indian state of West Bengal. Ancient Rarh region was divided into several smaller regions – Kankagrambhukti, Bardhamanbhukti and Dandabhukti, as part of the Gupta Empire.

topPlaces.name: Goda, Purba Bardhaman
topPlaces.description: Goda is a census town in Burdwan I CD Block in Bardhaman Sadar North subdivision of Purba Bardhaman district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Christ Church Bardhaman
topPlaces.description: Christ Church Bardhaman is the oldest Church of undivided Bardhaman district. It is situated beside Grand Trunk Road in Bardhaman in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Curzon Gate, Bardhaman
topPlaces.description: The Curzon Gate, also known as the Bijay Toran, is a prominent landmark in Bardhaman city in Purba Bardhaman district in West Bengal, India. It is located at the crossing of Bijoy Chand Road and Grand Trunk Road.

topPlaces.name: Bardhaman
topPlaces.description: Bardhaman (, Bengali: [ˈbɔrˌd̪ʱo.man]), officially Bardhaman Sadar, is a city and municipality located on the northern bank of the Damodar river in the state of West Bengal, India. It is the headquarters of Purba Bardhaman district, having become a district capital during the period of British rule.

topPlaces.name: Bahir Sarbamangala
topPlaces.description: Bahir Sarbamangala is a census town in Burdwan I CD Block in Bardhaman Sadar North subdivision of Purba Bardhaman district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Nari, Purba Bardhaman
topPlaces.description: Nari is a census town in Burdwan I CD Block in Bardhaman Sadar North subdivision of Purba Bardhaman district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Mirzapur, Bardhaman
topPlaces.description: Mirzapur is a census town in Burdwan I CD Block in Bardhaman Sadar North subdivision of Purba Bardhaman district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ramnabagan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bardhamanbhukti, Goda, Purba Bardhaman, Christ Church Bardhaman, Curzon Gate, Bardhaman, Bardhaman.
